channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: Russian lawmakers endorse bill to ban adoptions by gender-transition countries
item.description: MOSCOW (AP) — Russia’s upper house of parliament on Wednesday endorsed a bill banning adoption of Russian children by citizens of countries where gender transitioning is legal. The Federation Council also approved bills that outlaw the spread of material that encourages people not to have children.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Pakistan reports new polio case in northwest, raising nationwide tally to 50 cases this year
item.description: Officials say Pakistan has detected one more polio case in the restive northwest bordering Afghanistan, raising the country’s tally of the infectious disease to 50 cases this year.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: UN accused of downplaying Hamas terrorists' use of Gaza hospitals as new report ignores important details
item.description: IDF reported encountering terrorists in hospitals but Hamas' health ministry refuses to admit the group's presence there. Critics say the U.N. and others are too quick to parrot Hamas talking points.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: US Embassy in Kyiv shuts down after receiving warning of 'significant' Russian air attack
item.description: KYIV, Ukraine (AP) — The U.S. Embassy in Kyiv said it has received warning of a potentially significant Russian air attack on Wednesday and would be closed as a precaution. In a statement, the embassy also instructed employees to shelter in place and also recommended that U.S.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Marcos says Indonesia has agreed to send Filipino death-row drug convict back to the Philippines
item.description: MANILA, Philippines (AP) — Philippine President Ferdinand Marcos Jr. said Wednesday that a deal has been reached for Indonesia to send back a Filipino death-row drug convict, who was nearly executed by firing squad but got a reprieve due to years of pleadings from Manila.
item.dc:creator: JIM GOMEZ AND NINIEK KARMINI, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Ukraine has seen success in building clean energy, which is harder for Russia to destroy
item.description: This year, Russian missile attacks have knocked out about 50% of Ukraine's power generation, a major threat as winter looms.
item.dc:creator: Peter Prengaman, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: North Korea sent more conventional weapons to Russia, South Korea says
item.description: SEOUL, South Korea (AP) — North Korea recently supplied additional artillery systems to Russia to support its war efforts against Ukraine, while some of the thousands of North Korean troops deployed in Russia have begun engaging in combat, South Korea’s spy agency told lawmakers Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: HYUNG-JIN KIM and KIM TONG-HYUNG, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Health advocates in Africa worry Trump will reimpose abortion 'gag rule' governing US aid
item.description: Women's health advocates in Africa are worried that U.S. President-elect Donald Trump will again invoke the so-called global gag rule, a policy that cuts off U.S. government funding for groups that offer abortion-related services.
item.dc:creator: Farai Mutsaka, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Argentina's Milei pivots to pragmatism in first talks with China's Xi after shaking up the G20
item.description: LA PAZ, Boliva (AP) — Last year, then-presidential candidate Javier Milei declared Argentina would not “make deals with communists” in China or Brazil, calling their leaders “murderers” and “thieves” in a bid to channel the populist energies of Donald Trump and other global far-right icons into a wi
item.dc:creator: ISABEL DEBRE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Australia's plan to ban children from social media proves popular and problematic
item.description: MELBOURNE, Australia (AP) — How do you remove children from the harms of social media? Politically the answer appears simple in Australia, but practically the solution could be far more difficult.
item.dc:creator: ROD McGUIRK, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: The dark energy pushing our universe apart may not be what it seems, scientists say
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Distant, ancient galaxies are giving scientists more hints that a mysterious force called dark energy may not be what they thought.
item.dc:creator: ADITHI RAMAKRISHNAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Hong Kong ex-publisher Jimmy Lai testifies he didn't ask Pence, Pompeo to take action against city
item.description: Former Hong Kong publisher Jimmy Lai is testifying in his defense at his landmark national security trial.
item.dc:creator: Kanis Leung, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Leader of Georgia's separatist region of Abkhazia resigns, ending unrest
item.description: Following days of unrest in which the opposition seized government buildings, the leader of Georgia's separatist region of Abkhazia has resigned.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Iran defies international pressure, increasing its stockpile of near weapons-grade uranium, UN says
item.description: VIENNA (AP) — Iran has defied international demands to rein in its nuclear program and has increased its stockpile of uranium enriched to near weapons-grade levels, according to a confidential report by the United Nations’ nuclear watchdog seen Tuesday by The Associated Press.
item.dc:creator: STEPHANIE LIECHTENSTEIN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Son of Norway's crown princess arrested on suspicion of rape
item.description: Marius Borg Høiby, the eldest son of Norway Crown Princess Mette-Marit, was arrested on suspicion of rape in Oslo late last night.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Sweden, Finland and Norway release new advice on surviving war amid concerns over Russia, Ukraine escalation
item.description: Sweden, Finland and Norway this week released detailed guidance for citizens on how to survive a crisis situation like extreme weather or war-like conditions that could hamper food or internet access.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: More Ukrainians want talks to end war with Russia: Gallup
item.description: More than half of Ukrainians want talks to end the war with Russia, according to Gallup polls released Tuesday, the war's 1,000th day.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, The Hill

item.title: 45 pro-democracy Hong Kong activists sentenced to up to 10 years in prison under China-backed law
item.description: Western countries are outraged after Hong Kong used a Chinese national security law to sentence 45 pro-democracy activists to up to 10 years in prison.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Norwegian mass killer Anders Behring Breivik seeks parole for a second time
item.description: Norwegian mass murderer Anders Behring Breivik, who killed 77 people in a bombing and shooting rampage in 2011, was in court on Tuesday for a parole hearing. It was the far-right extremist's second bid for freedom after spending more than a decade behind bars.
item.dc:creator: OSLO, Norway, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: India overtakes China as top source of international students at US colleges
item.description: For the 2023-2024 school year, India saw a massive 23 percent increase in the number of college students sent to America.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, NewsNation

item.title: Bolton: Moscow, Beijing should feel ‘a lot of relief’ if Gabbard is confirmed as DNI
item.description: Former national security adviser John Bolton said that Russia and China should feel “a lot of relief” if former Rep. Tulsi Gabbard (Hawaii) is confirmed as President-elect Trump’s choice for director of national intelligence. “She’s not capable of doing it,” Bolton said Sunday on CNN.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, NewsNation

item.title: Netanyahu confirms Israel strike against Iran hit nuke program during October retaliatory strikes
item.description: Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu confirmed that part of Iran's nuclear program was degraded after IDF strikes on military facilities in and around Tehran in October.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Top House Democrat: Gabbard 'a national security threat' to US
item.description: Rep. Gregory Meeks (D-N.Y.), the ranking member on the House Foreign Affairs Committee, said Monday that former Democratic Rep. Tulsi Gabbard (Hawaii), now a Republican and President-elect Trump’s pick for director of national intelligence, is “a national security threat” to the United States.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, The Hill

item.title: Thune threatens International Criminal Court with sanctions if it doesn’t drop Netanyahu warrant for arrest
item.description: Sen. John Thune said he will go after the International Criminal Court with sanctions once the GOP take the majority in the Senate come January if the court does not drop its case against Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Biden announces new sanctions against 'extremist' Israeli settlers
item.description: The Biden administration on Monday announced sanctions against what it described as Israeli citizens and entities involved in the extremist settler movement in the West Bank, part of U.S. efforts to curb escalating violence targeting Palestinians in the territory.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, The Hill

item.title: Israeli strike kills Hezbollah spokesman in Beirut; at least 30 dead in Gaza airstrikes
item.description: A rare Israeli strike in central Beirut has killed the chief spokesman for the Hezbollah militant group. It was the latest in targeted killings of senior Hezbollah officials.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: From the Amazon rainforest, Biden declares nobody can reverse US progress on clean energy
item.description: MANAUS, Brazil (AP) — Speaking from the Amazon rainforest, President Joe Biden declared Sunday that there’s no going back in America’s “clean energy revolution” even as the incoming Trump administration vows to spur fossil fuel production and scale back efforts against climate change.
item.dc:creator: AAMER MADHANI, GABRIELA SÁ PESSOA and COLLEEN LONG, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: VIDEO: Severe flight turbulence on Florida-bound flight sends person airborne, requires plane inspection
item.description: A Miami-bound flight turned frightening after the plane experienced severe turbulence, causing a diversion and requiring inspection of the plane.
item.dc:creator: Sara Filips, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Zelenskyy: Russia using Iranian weapons in 'massive' attack targeting energy infrastructure
item.description: Ukrainian forces say Russia launched over 200 drones and missiles against the nation's power and energy infrastructure this weekend.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: 2,000-year-old Roman road discovered by archaeologists in London
item.description: An ancient Roman road was recently discovered by archaeologists in London. The 2,000-year-old road was located beneath modern-day Old Kent Road.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump looms large as Biden set to meet China's Xi during Latin America summits
item.description: President Biden is in Latin America for the APEC and G-20 summits, in what is likely his last trip as president. The back-to-back summits come as an impending second Trump presidency is near, which may rattle some world leaders.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Ukrainian troops train for trench warfare near France's WWI battlefields
item.description: Around 2,000 Ukrainian soldiers are training in France's eastern Marne region, where German and French armies battled each other during World War I.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Moo Deng, Thailand's baby pygmy hippo, gets official song in 4 languages
item.description: In case you can’t get enough of the little pygmy hippo Moo Deng from Thailand, there's now an official song featuring the internet's favorite baby animal — released in four languages for her global fans.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: US government worker charged with leaking classified documents on Israel's plans to strike Iran
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — A man who worked for the U.S. government has been charged with leaking classified information assessing Israel's earlier plans to attack Iran, according to court papers filed Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: ERIC TUCKER, Associated Press, NewsNation
